messages.content: I am getting an error on this: =IFS(H4<>L4,E4 & " Incomplete", L4="", E4 & " No Entry", "")

The error is caused by using the IFS function incorrectly. In the IFS function, each condition and its corresponding result should be separated by commas. However, in your formula, you used a comma after `H4<>L4` condition, but you didn't provide a result for that condition before moving to the next condition. 

To fix the error, you can modify your formula to include a condition and result for that specific case. Here's the corrected formula:

```
=IFS(H4<>L4, E4 & " Incomplete", L4="", E4 & " No Entry", TRUE, "")
```

In this updated formula, the last condition `TRUE` is used as a catch-all for all other cases where none of the previous conditions are met. If none of the previous conditions are met (i.e., `H4<>L4` is False and L4 is not blank), it will return an empty string ("").
how can I write this formula to show date and time: schedulePrint.Range("C6").Value = Date

To show both the date and time in your formula, you can use the NOW function instead of the DATE function. Here's the updated formula:

```python
schedulePrint.Range("C6").Value = Now
```

This formula will assign the current date and time to the cell C6.

Apologies for the confusion. It seems you want to assign the current date and time to the cell C6 in VBA code. Here's the corrected code for that:

```vba
schedulePrint.Range("C6").Value = Now
```

This VBA code will assign the current date and time to the cell C6 in the specified worksheet (`schedulePrint`).

In my range H3:H13 will always have a value.
Occassionally L3:13 will have some empty cells.

Can you please help me to write an IF statement that I can enter in A3 (and drag down to A13) that does the following:

If L3<>"" AND L3=H3 then the value in A3 = E3 & " Completed"
If L3<>"" AND L3<H3 then the value in A3 = E3 & " Incomplete"
If L3="" AND H3<>"" then the value in A3 = E3

Certainly! Here's an IF statement that you can use in cell A3 and drag down to A13 to achieve your desired results:

```excel
=IF(AND(L3<>"",L3=H3), E3 & " Completed", IF(AND(L3<>"",L3<H3), E3 & " Incomplete", IF(AND(L3="",H3<>""), E3, "")))
```

This formula uses nested IF statements along with the AND function to check multiple conditions and assign the corresponding value to cell A3. 

Make sure to adjust the cell references (`L3`, `H3`, `E3`, etc.) as per your actual data range. After entering the formula in A3, you can drag it down to auto-fill the formula in cells A4 to A13.
The last part of the formula is not working: IF(AND(L3="",H3<>""), E3, ""

Apologies for the confusion. If you want to check if L3 is empty and H3 is not empty, and assign the value of E3 to cell A3, you can use the following formula:

```
=IF(AND(L3="", H3<>""), E3, "")
```

Ensure you adjust the cell references `L3`, `H3`, and `E3` as per your specific data range.
In row 4 I have the formula '=IF(AND(L4<>"",L4=H4), E4 & " Completed", IF(AND(L4<>"",L4<H4), E4 & " Incomplete", IF(AND(L4=""), E4, "")))'. L4 is empty but the formula is not recognising this

If the formula is not recognizing L4 as empty, it could be due to leading or trailing spaces in the cell. In order to handle this situation, you can use the TRIM function to remove any extra spaces before checking if L4 is empty.

You can modify your formula as follows:

```
=IF(AND(TRIM(L4)="", H4<>""), E4, IF(AND(TRIM(L4)<>"", L4=H4), E4 & " Completed", IF(AND(TRIM(L4)<>"", L4<H4), E4 & " Incomplete", "")))
```

By using the TRIM function, any leading or trailing spaces in L4 will be removed before checking if it is empty. This should help the formula recognize the empty cell correctly.
It still does not recognise: TRIM(L4)=""), E4

Apologies for the confusion. If the formula is still not recognizing L4 as empty, you can try using the ISBLANK function to check if L4 is empty.

You can modify your formula as follows:

```
=IF(AND(ISBLANK(L4), H4<>""), E4, IF(AND(L4<>"", L4=H4), E4 & " Completed", IF(AND(L4<>"", L4<H4), E4 & " Incomplete", "")))
```

By using the ISBLANK function, it directly checks if L4 is a blank cell. If it is blank, it will assign the value of E4 to A4.

Make sure to adjust the cell references (`L4`, `H4`, `E4`, etc.) as per your actual data range.
Soes not recognise that L4 is blank

Apologies for the continued confusion. If the ISBLANK function is not recognizing L4 as blank, it's possible that there may be some non-visible characters or formatting in the cell. In this case, you can use the LEN function to check the length of the cell value, instead of ISBLANK function.

You can modify your formula as follows:

```
=IF(AND(LEN(L4)=0, H4<>""), E4, IF(AND(L4<>"", L4=H4), E4 & " Completed", IF(AND(L4<>"", L4<H4), E4 & " Incomplete", "")))
```

By using the LEN function along with the condition `LEN(L4)=0`, it checks if the length of the cell value is zero, indicating that L4 is empty. If it is empty, it will assign the value of E4 to A4.

Make sure to adjust the cell references (`L4`, `H4`, `E4`, etc.) as per your actual data range.
still not working

Apologies for the confusion. If the LEN function is not working as expected, there may be other issues causing the formula to not recognize the blank cell. 

In such cases, it's helpful to check the cell formatting. If you have applied specific formatting to L4, it may affect the check for blank cells. Make sure the cell is set to the default General format.

Additionally, you can try using the following formula, which combines ISBLANK and IFERROR functions:

```
=IFERROR(IF(AND(ISBLANK(L4), H4<>""), E4, IF(AND(L4<>"", L4=H4), E4 & " Completed", IF(AND(L4<>"", L4<H4), E4 & " Incomplete", ""))), "")
```

This updated formula uses ISBLANK function along with IFERROR function to handle any errors that might be occurring. If any error is encountered, it will return an empty string ("").

Make sure to adjust the cell references (`L4`, `H4`, `E4`, etc.) as per your actual data range.
In A4 there are no errors, it just gives me the value 'AS4 Incomplete' instead of 'AS4'

Apologies for the confusion. If you want to show just the value "AS4" in cell A4 instead of "AS4 Incomplete", you can adjust the formula as follows:

```
=IF(AND(ISBLANK(L4), H4<>""), E4, IF(AND(L4<>"", L4=H4), E4 & " Completed", IF(AND(L4<>"", L4<H4), E4, "")))
```

By removing the text " Incomplete" from the second condition, the formula will only append "Completed" or return the value in E4 without any additional text.

Make sure to adjust the cell references (`L4`, `H4`, `E4`, etc.) as per your actual data range.
Can it not provide three True conditions: AS4 Completed, AS4 Incomplete, AS4

I apologize for the misunderstanding. If you'd like to have three possible outcomes in the formula ("AS4 Completed", "AS4 Incomplete", or "AS4") based on the conditions provided, you can modify the formula as shown below:

```
=IF(AND(ISBLANK(L4), H4<>""), E4, IF(AND(L4<>"", L4=H4), E4 & " Completed", IF(AND(L4<>"", L4<H4), E4 & " Incomplete", E4)))
```

With this modification, if L4 is empty and H4 is not empty, cell A4 will display the value in E4. If L4 is not empty and matches H4, cell A4 will display the value in E4 followed by "Completed". If L4 is not empty and is less than H4, cell A4 will display the value in E4 followed by "Incomplete". If none of the conditions are met, cell A4 will show the value in E4.

Please ensure to adjust the cell references (`L4`, `H4`, `E4`, etc.) according to your specific data range.
It appears that it can not handle the three conditions

Apologies for the confusion. If you want to handle three conditions ("AS4 Completed", "AS4 Incomplete", or "AS4"), you can use the following formula:

```
=IF(L4<>"", IF(L4=H4, E4 & " Completed", IF(L4<H4, E4 & " Incomplete", E4)), "")
```

In this formula, if L4 is not empty, it will check for the two conditions: L4 equals H4 and L4 is less than H4. If L4 = H4, it will display "E4 Completed". If L4 < H4, it will display "E4 Incomplete". If neither condition is met (L4 is neither equal nor less than H4), it will simply display the value in E4.

If L4 is empty, the formula will return an empty string ("").

Please adjust the cell references (`L4`, `H4`, `E4`, etc.) based on your actual data range.
